# @graphyn/code llms.txt

## Repository Identity
- Project: `@graphyn/code`
- Type: multi-agent developer CLI (Mission Control + orchestration)
- Primary role: natural-language dev workflows, agent orchestration, and runtime integrations

## Backbone Operating Line
- Wiring-first: keep real backend/runtime pathways; no fake orchestration success.
- Source-anchored planning: map claims to concrete files/commands.
- Reporting format: phase, current step, completed, remaining, blockers.

## Stack
- Node.js CLI package (`graphyn` binary)
- TypeScript build pipeline (`tsc`)
- Commander/inquirer/chalk/ora terminal UX
- Multi-agent orchestration modules + streaming clients

## Critical Paths
- Entry points: `src/index.ts`, `src/cli.ts`, `src/unified-cli.ts`
- Orchestration: `src/orchestrator/*`, `src/coordinator/*`
- Engine/service layers: `src/engines/*`, `src/services/*`
- Agent/rules/config: `src/agents/*`, `src/config/*`
- Runtime interfaces: `src/console/*`, `src/streaming/*`, `src/sdk/*`

## Local Commands
- Install: `pnpm install` (or npm per local workflow)
- Build: `npm run build`
- Dev: `npm run dev`
- Package validation: `npm run test:package`

## Agent Workflow Checklist
1. Scope target command path before editing (`init`, `thread`, `agent`, orchestration commands).
2. Keep CLI contract stable (`graphyn` executable + flags/subcommands).
3. Run `npm run build` after changes; run package test when touching release paths.
4. Include verification output excerpts before completion claims.

## Dependency Docs (Reference)
- Commander docs: https://github.com/tj/commander.js#readme
- Inquirer docs: https://github.com/SBoudrias/Inquirer.js#readme
- Chalk docs: https://github.com/chalk/chalk#readme
- Ora docs: https://github.com/sindresorhus/ora#readme
- EventSource spec/docs: https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/EventSource
- Node.js docs: https://nodejs.org/docs/latest-v22.x/api/

## Execution Guardrails
- Preserve non-interactive and CI-safe command behavior where supported.
- Avoid regressions in stream/orchestrator error handling paths.
- Keep public package surface aligned with README usage examples.
